name: Abdoulaye DIALLO
description_1: Hello! I'm Abdoulaye, a passionate software developer who loves participating in innovative projects as well as creating my own. The last project I worked on was a fullstack application using Angular for the frontend and Java(Quarkus) for the backend. This project was a web platform, used for IoT data integration and visualization in Smart City and smart building contexts. I developed a generic booking module (rooms, parking spots) for smart buildings and integrated it into the UDP. As well as structuring the database, using MongoBD, and the UI for managing and booking the resources in smart buildings.
description_2: With a background in Computer Science, having just completed my end-of-studies internship with the Inno Lab team at Atos Pessac, completing my Master's degree in Computer Science with a specialization in Software Engineering, I am eager to start work. I am experienced with various technologies and frameworks. I have recently taken an interest in RootMe, and try to complete challenges to improve my cybersecurity skills. I remain curious and ready to gain more skills in various other areas.
description_3: Feel free to explore my portfolio to learn more about my projects and skills. If you have any questions or would like to connect, don't hesitate to reach out through the contact section. Thank you for visiting my portfolio!

project_0_description: I developed this portfolio website using Angular for the frontend and Google Sheets for the backend, giving me a serverless backend. I am using two sheets on google sheets, one for the stats and one for the contact page. To make this work a script is deployed from Google Sheets, which the Angular frontend connects to in order to send and receive data. The website showcases my projects, skills, and experience in a visually appealing way.
project_1_title: Smart Building — Generic Booking Module (UDP Vertical)
project_1_description: I developed a generic booking module (rooms, parking spots) integrated into UDP during my internship. I also structured the database and UI for managing and booking resources in smart buildings. And I created a reusable component for other verticals of the platform.
project_2_description: As a duo we developed a graphical application similar to Paint, implementing several design patterns. The app allows the user to play around with predefined shapes via a drag-and-drop feature, as well as resizing, moving, and combining shapes.
project_3_description: I developed two basic desktop applications using Python and Flask: the MT940 App and the MT940 Sort Folder App. These applications are designed to facilitate the processing and sorting of financial transaction files, making it easier to manage and organize large sets of data. The MT940 App processes files generated by the bank's software, modifies specific details like BIC numbers, and produces a structured output. The MT940 Sort Folder App takes these processed files and further organizes them into a sorted structure based on clients and currencies.
project_4_description: As a team of 3, we developed a 2D civilization game using Java, where we often physically coded together, and met up often. The goal of the game was to manage resources, build structures, and expand the player's civilization. Furthermore, the inhabitants of the civilization consume resources periodically, adding a layer of strategy to resource management. The player must balance resource generation and consumption to ensure the survival and growth of their civilization. The player loses when they no longer have food resources to feed their inhabitants. The goal of this project, for us as developers, was to implement the Domain Driven Design(DDD). We designed the game around its core domain, focusing on the essential aspects of civilization management and resource allocation. The DDD is ideal for this type of project, as it allows for a clear separation of concerns and a better understanding of the domain logic.
project_5_description: In a team of 3, we developed a puzzle game where the goal is to light up all cells on a grid by placing bulbs according to specific rules. The game includes multiple grids of increasing difficulty and a user-friendly interface. It was developed in C, and features a graphical interface using the SDL library.
upcoming_project_1_description: A web application that creates a mosaic of posts(images, videos) and/or comments that a user saved on their Reddit account. Users log in with their Reddit credentials, and the app will fetch their saved posts to generate a mosaic of reddit posts and/or comments. Features will include: Search option, sort by subreddit, sort by type (image, comment, video), sort by date, display the reddit post in the webpage, with the option to open it directly on reddit. A few versions of this idea exist, but I plan to add more features and provide a different user experience.
experience_1_title: Software Developer Intern - Inno Lab Team
experience_1_description: I participated in the development of the UDP (Universal Data Platform) web platform, used for IoT data integration and visualization in Smart City and Smart Building contexts. Implemented a generic booking module for managing resources (rooms, parking spaces) in smart buildings.
experience_2_description: I contributed to the creation of two web apps to improve the processing of banking documents. And participated in designing technical solutions to automate certain administrative tasks.
experience_3_description: I would provide tutoring sessions for undergraduate computer science students. This meant answering questions and helping with exercises and project understanding.
